cv.target_role: Editor, Music & Culture (International & Hip-Hop) — Spotify, Mumbai

home.intro_markdown:
I am applying for **Editor, Music & Culture (International & Hip-Hop)** in Mumbai. This site is a working portfolio — not a dashboard demo — built from my own listening history and editorial judgment.

Recent rotation leans club and electronic; long-term taste runs through India-linked hip-hop and global crossovers. Each section shows how I would spot momentum, programme lanes, and sequence a set with a point of view.

role_fit.markdown:
# Role Fit — Editor, Music & Culture (International & Hip-Hop) — Spotify, Mumbai

**Sumit Redu** · Sierra Romeo

Senior Emissions Analyst @ ShipWatch | IIM Mumbai MBA '26

Mumbai, Maharashtra, India · sumitredu@hotmail.com | +91-9529307047

[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sumit-redu-643a66196/)



*Personal listening data used with authorization. Not affiliated with Spotify; no access to internal chart or audience data.*



### Summary

~15 years professional experience: 12 years at sea with Maersk, emissions analytics since 2023, MBA in progress, plus shipped analytics products.

Recent plays skew **club/electronic**; long-term taste includes **India ↔ global hip-hop** (OtaaL, Hanumankind, Shubh, AP Dhillon, Prateek Kuhad, NAALAYAK). DJ work in Delhi and Ajmer (2021–2023) and this portfolio show culture-first judgment backed by listening evidence.

Reconciling sensor data with reports and building alert rules is not unlike reading listen patterns and playlist cohesion here.

Listening footprint: Spotify since 2017 (~9 yrs); 2226 unique songs, 2062 artists across top-track windows.

Library exports: 17084 tracks across 88 playlists — genres and audio features from personal exports, not live chart data.


---


## What I'll do


### Spot trends and emerging artists

Recent rotation: G-Eazy, Fred again.., &ME, Skepta, PlaqueBoyMax. Short-term frontier: Banjaare, The Weeknd, Daft Punk, G-Eazy, The Fray, &ME. Busiest discovery day in this window: **2026-06-06** (39 new artists).

Hip-hop / indie lane in current watchlist: Skepta, PlaqueBoyMax, J. Cole, Kid Cudi, Bas. Track momentum: Victory Lap, Please Don't Go, Starting Again.

At ShipWatch I track regulatory signals and turn them into playbooks — the same habit of watching velocity before something becomes obvious.


### Curate with sequencing and voice

Playlist proof: **Project Alpha** (cohesion 100, discovery 0); **Goa 25** (cohesion 68, discovery 19); **B-78** (cohesion 44, discovery 26). Editorial concepts on Curate include *Breakout Watch*, *Global Hip-Hop: India Entry*, *Indian Hip-Hop Export*, *Club Crossover*, and *Late Night Mumbai Flow*.

Years of voyage sequencing and emissions reporting taught me how to build a set with a clear arc: opener, lift, peak, release.


### Bridge global music and Indian audiences

Long-term anchors: **OtaaL, Hanumankind, Shubh, AP Dhillon, Prateek Kuhad, NAALAYAK**. Recent club/electronic rotation is a natural bridge into India-relevant slots.

Global-facing rotation (Daft Punk, Black Coffee, Keinemusik) — natural candidates for India entry programming.

India-linked repeat plays (Prateek Kuhad, NAALAYAK, Sonu Nigam) — export and diaspora lanes.

Based in Mumbai for the role; my listening spans global club/electronic rotation and desi hip-hop in long-term taste.


### Use data and audience insight

Repeat affinity across time windows: Fred again.., Jamie T, Dennis Lloyd, Khalid — a stable core to build programming around.

Recent shift toward G-Eazy, Mac Miller, &ME — discovery momentum in the short window.

41% of top tracks from the last two years — recency-heavy taste.

Current listening mode: **discovery/listening exploration**.

Reconciling sensor data with reports and building alert rules is not unlike reading listen patterns and playlist cohesion here. Also built [FPL analytics](https://fpl-lac.vercel.app); IIM certifications in Generative AI and data-driven decision-making.


### Collaborate across editorial, marketing, and product

Used to working with verifiers, platform engineers, and fleet clients; comfortable presenting analysis to non-technical stakeholders.


### Tell stories beyond playlists

Between 2021 and 2023, after stepping back from the maritime track, I rebuilt creatively through DJ classes and long hours of practice in Delhi, with club and private gigs in Delhi and Ajmer. That stretch taught me to read a room, sequence energy, and notice what people lean into early — instincts I now bring to data-led editorial work.

IIM Mumbai MBA; maritime sustainability writing on FuelEU / EU ETS (LinkedIn).


## How I match the role


| Requirement | Match |

|-------------|-------|

| 6+ years in music, media, or culture-adjacent work | Spotify since 2017 (~9 yrs); 2226 unique songs, 2062 artists across top-track windows. DJ work in Delhi & Ajmer (2021–2023); 12 years Maersk; analytics + FPL app + this lab. |

| Global culture, hip-hop, India | Club/electronic in recent plays; desi hip-hop in long-term taste (OtaaL, Hanumankind, Shubh, AP Dhillon, Prateek Kuhad, NAALAYAK). |

| Data-led decisions | ShipWatch/GeoServe analytics; Python dashboards; this lab. |

| English (+ Indian languages a plus) | Professional English; Hindi/regional context via India lanes. |

| Collaboration | Cross-functional maritime tech, verifier partnerships, MBA cohort. |


## Location


Mumbai — matches role location; open to hybrid per JD.


## Experience



- ShipWatch (Jul 2025–present), Mumbai — Senior Emissions Analyst: FuelEU Maritime, EU MRV/ETS, IMO DCS–CII; audit-ready workflows, evidence packs, and analyst playbooks for fleet clients.

- GeoServe (Apr 2024–Jul 2025) — Senior Emissions Analyst / Emissions Officer: FuelEU calculator in GeoPerform VPS; DNV Veracity API integration (~30% less manual processing).

- Scorpio India (Apr 2023–Mar 2024) — Emission Monitoring Officer: voyage and emissions data with DNV; ~20% fewer data discrepancies.

- A.P. Moller–Maersk (Jan 2009–Mar 2021) — Dual Cadet → Third Officer → Second Officer: voyage planning, bridge operations, fuel optimisation.

- Career transition (Mar 2021–Apr 2023) — DJ classes and practice in Delhi; club and private gigs in Delhi and Ajmer.

- IIM Mumbai — Executive MBA, Logistics Supply Chain & Maritime Management (2024–2026, completing Mar 2026).

- Side projects — FPL analytics app (https://fpl-lac.vercel.app); Sierra Romeo Editorial Intelligence Lab (this dashboard).


## Certifications



- Generative AI for Data-Driven Business Decision-Making, IIM Mumbai (Apr 2025)

- Modelling Digital Supply Chain Twins (anyLogistix), IIM Mumbai (May 2025)

- Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate, Coursera (2021–2022)


---

*Portfolio demonstration only — not affiliated with Spotify. Listening metrics are personal heuristics, not official Spotify KPIs.*

Breakout Watch: Pre-Mainstream.narrative:
**Breakout Watch: Pre-Mainstream**

Opens on **Lights Burn Dimmer** (Fred again.., Jamie T) — opener, setting the room without rushing the peak.

Builds through Banjaare, Fred again.., SOAK, Lucca Saettone.

Peak: **TRIPPIN** (BUNT., Mapei).

Closes with **Haseen** (Talwiinder, NDS, Rippy Grewal) — cooldown.
